parts.text:
You are an expert frontend engineer and UI designer.

Create a modern landing page for an AI Voice Agent product inspired by the "Background Paths" design from Kokonut UI.
this is the desing that i wnat
http://cdn.21st.dev/bundled/675.html?theme=dark&dark=true

with animation but for my voice agnet

Design Requirements:

STYLE

* Dark futuristic theme
* Minimal and clean layout
* Soft glowing gradients
* Animated curved SVG paths flowing across the background
* Subtle motion effects and smooth transitions
* Modern AI startup aesthetic

HERO SECTION
Center aligned hero section with:

* Large headline introducing the voice agent
* Short description explaining what the voice agent does
* Primary CTA button (Try Demo)
* Secondary CTA (View Docs)

BACKGROUND

* Animated curved path lines flowing across the screen
* Thin glowing lines
* Gradient glow effects
* Slight blur and parallax motion
* The background animation should feel futuristic and calm

NAVBAR
Top navigation with:

* Logo
* Features
* Demo
* Pricing
* GitHub
* CTA button (Start Free)

FEATURE SECTION
3–4 feature cards explaining the voice agent:

* Real-time voice conversation
* Natural AI responses
* API integration
* Fast latency

DEMO SECTION
Include a demo card with:

* Microphone button
* Voice wave animation
* "Talk to the agent" UI

DESIGN SYSTEM
Use:

* React / Next.js
* Tailwind CSS
* Framer Motion animations
* Clean component structure

VISUAL DETAILS

* Rounded UI elements
* Soft shadows
* Glassmorphism cards
* Smooth hover animations
* Responsive layout

IMPORTANT
The background must replicate the "curved animated path lines" style from Kokonut UI Background Paths.

OUTPUT
Provide the full landing page code and component structure.

[CODE SUMMARY: React/Next.js landing page for AI Voice Agent with dark futuristic theme. Components: AnimatedBackground with SVG curved paths and CSS keyframe animations, Navbar with mobile menu and scroll effects, FeatureCard with glassmorphism and hover animations, DemoSection with interactive microphone button and voice wave visualization, StatsSection, CTA section, Footer. Uses Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion, lucide-react icons. Responsive design with mobile-first approach.]

https://p.superdesign.dev/draft/6e168f07-7eec-42e3-b00b-731466d2fe24

[CODE SUMMARY: Enhanced React landing page for Aura Voice Agent with Kokonut UI-inspired animated background paths, scroll-aware navbar, expanded hero with gradient text, StatsSection for metrics, 6 feature cards with varied gradients, interactive DemoSection with dynamic audio wave visualization, polished CTA section, multi-column footer. Uses Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion, lucide-react. Fully responsive with glassmorphism, rounded UI, subtle hover effects.]

[CODE SUMMARY: Fixed lucide-react import error (Waveform -> AudioWaveform). Complete React component for Aura Voice Agent landing page: AnimatedBackground with SVG curved paths, Navbar with mobile menu, VoiceWave animation component, FeatureCard/UseCaseCard with glassmorphism, interactive DemoSection with transcript, full page structure (Hero, Social Proof, Features, Demo, Use Cases, CTA, Footer). Uses Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion, lucide-react.]

# Knowledge Base

## The content from [File](Pasted_Text_1772960415035.txt):

[HTML content from SuperDesign preview for Aura Voice Agent landing page including Tailwind CSS, Iconify icons, custom fonts (Cabinet Grotesk, Satoshi), CSS animations for voice-wave and glass-card effects, SuperDesign runtime scripts for component registration and screenshot capture, and full HTML structure with Navbar, Hero, Social Proof, Features, Demo, Use Cases, CTA, and Footer sections]

[CODE SUMMARY: React implementation of Aura Voice Agent landing page based on SuperDesign HTML reference. Components: AnimatedBackground with SVG curved path animations, VoiceWave with pulse animation, Navbar with mobile menu, FeatureCard/UseCaseCard with glassmorphism hover effects, DemoSection with interactive transcript, complete page structure matching reference design. Uses Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion, lucide-react. Responsive layout, gradient text, subtle motion transitions.]
did you know about mirofish?

[SUMMARY] MiroFish: open-source AI prediction engine using multi-agent swarm intelligence. Workflow: seed input→knowledge graph→agent creation→simulation→analysis. Use cases: market analysis, public opinion forecasting, strategy testing, creative applications. Notable: GitHub open-source, rapid growth, democratizes foresight. Warning: approach predictions with skepticism due to real-world unpredictability.
i have setpu and run it locally , now i wnat to try it with data but i did not know what shoud i uplaod and what shoud i describe predituion reqiment in neturel language

[SUMMARY] Seed materials: PDF/Markdown/TXT/CSV-XLSX formats. Good seeds: news, reports, policy drafts, financial briefings, fiction with personas/pain points. Prompt template: [Event] happened→how do [groups] react over [timeframe]? + specific questions. Tips: anchor to specific event, focus on behaviors/narratives, include segment contrasts, end with conversion question. Workflow: prepare seed doc→write prompt→upload→run. Local tips: valid LLM API keys in .env, start small (10-20 agents), GraphRAG build 7-10min once per doc.

can u create one .md file about iran us war and gold price and then we will ask to pedcit gold price in documenet metnion all current new about gold , i wnat to predcit gold price so creat tha level of doc

[SUMMARY] Created iran_us_gold_prediction_seed.md with: Executive summary (gold ~$4,400-$5,000/oz baseline). Geopolitical context: Iran-US flashpoints, 3 scenarios (diplomacy 25%: $4,100-$4,200; status quo 50%: $4,400-$4,600; escalation 25%: $4,900-$5,200). Fundamentals: drivers matrix (geopolitical risk, DXY, Fed policy, central bank buying, real yields, ETF flows), technical levels (resistance $5,050/$5,200/$5,400; support $4,400/$4,150/$3,900). Agent personas: institutional (hedge fund PM, central bank trader, CTA), retail (US retail, Indian buyer, crypto trader), media (Bloomberg, Twitter, state media). Shock events with probabilities/impacts. Prediction questions: price trajectory, narrative evolution, agent behavior, cross-asset spillovers, policy implications. Usage: upload file + prompt example + iterate steps. Local notes: LLM API keys, ≥8GB RAM for 50+ agents, GraphRAG 7-12min, CSV option for historical data.

parts.text:
We want engineers who build things, break things, and have opinions about both.

This is not a leetcode test. We don't care if you can reverse a binary tree on a whiteboard. We care if you can debug a production issue at 11 pm with incomplete logs and a PM pinging you non stop.

A few ground rules:

Be honest. Messy, real answers beat polished, AI-generated ones.
We can tell when answers are written by AI. Don't do it. Seriously.
There are no trick questions. We just want to hear how you actually think.
This should take 10-15 minutes. If you're spending an hour, you're overthinking it.
Let's go. Type NA if applying for a non engineering role.

Send us a link to something you've built that you are proud of: a repo, a deployed build, even a messy side project. In 2-3 lines, tell us what was the hardest part of building it.
*
You push code on Friday evening. Monday morning, 3 users report the app is

No errors in logs. What's your actual first move? Not the textbook answer, your real one.
*
What's a tool, framework, or

that's popular right now that you think is overrated? Be specific and tell us why.
*
Write a function in any language that takes a list of timestamps (ISO format) and returns the longest gap between consecutive events. Don't Google it, don't use AI. Messy code is fine and preferred. 
*
You join a 2-year-old codebase with zero documentation, one original dev who left, and a PM who wants a new feature by next week. Walk us through your first 48 hours.
*

[ASSISTANT SUMMARY] Provided human-style answers for five engineering screening questions. Included project description, debugging approach, opinion on over-engineering frameworks, JavaScript function for longest timestamp gap, and plan for navigating undocumented codebase. Code block replaced with summary.
[CODE SUMMARY: JavaScript function converting ISO timestamps to ms, sorting, computing max diff]

Write a function in any language that takes a list of timestamps (ISO format) and returns the longest gap between consecutive events. Don't Google it, don't use AI. Messy code is fine and preferred. 
*

[CODE SUMMARY: JS function mapping timestamps to numbers, sorting, loop computing max gap]

This is the most important section. Please do not use AI to answer these, I assure you we'll know.
Why do you want to work at 360 Labs? The more honest you are, the better. 
*
If you were given infinite access to investment, what product/service would you build and why?
*
What important truth do very few people agree with you on? 
*
What do you think is the meaning of life? Have you found a purpose and if so what is it? 
*

Provided human sounding answers with slight grammar imperfections explaining motivation to work at 360 Labs, idea for AI collaborative system, belief about curiosity over talent, and personal meaning of life focused on learning and building.
